creator: Emily English
source_text:
Sticky Honey Chicken with Charred Peach Salsa

Cook once, eat twice. This is exactly the kind of recipe I love for summer meal prep. It tastes just as good packed into lunch boxes for the next day. 

Serves 4 535 kcal & 30g protein per serving 
200g basmati rice, dry weight
500g boneless, skinless chicken thighs
1 tbsp honey 2 garlic cloves, finely chopped or grated 1 heaped tsp smoked paprika Juice of ½ lemon
1 tsp any mustard 1/2 tsp finely chopped fresh rosemary 
For the charred peach salsa
2 ripe but firm peaches, diced 1 medium avocado, diced ½ small red onion, finely diced Juice of ½ lemon 1 tsp honey Pinch of chilli flakes 2 tsp extra virgin olive oil
Large handful cherry tomatoes, diced (about 120g) Large handful fresh basil, torn 60g feta, crumbled, optional

Add the chicken thighs to a bowl with the honey, garlic, smoked paprika, mustard, lemon juice, rosemary and plenty of salt and pepper. Toss until the chicken is evenly coated. Leave to marinate whilst you prepare the rest. 

Cook the rice according to the packet instructions.

Heat a frying pan over a high heat. Add the diced peaches with honey & chilli flakes until charred around the edges. Try not to move them around too much so they have time to caramelise. Leave to cool slightly in a bowl. Add the red onion, avocado, lemon juice, olive oil and torn basil to the peaches. Season, crumble in the feta if using. Taste and adjust to your pallet. It should be bright, punchy and fresh. 

Preheat the air fryer to 200°C or heat the grill to its highest setting. Arrange the chicken in a single layer and air fry for 15 to 18 minutes, turning halfway, until cooked through with sticky, charred edges. Alternatively, grill for 7 to 9 minutes on each side. Leave the chicken to rest for a few minutes before slicing.

Divide the rice between four bowls or lunch boxes, then top with the sliced sticky chicken and charred peach salsa. For meal prep, keep the salsa in if eating cold or store separately. 

#healthyrecipes #easyrecipe #highprotein #mealprep
